headline: First-ever evidence-based baseline (Night 9): April 24 5th Circuit ruling vacated SB4 injunction, allowing state police to arrest persons suspected of illegal border entry and order removal to Mexico — state-level parallel deportation system activated; April 22 Houston ordinance gutted under Abbott $110M+ funding coercion; Texas holds 18,734 ICE detainees (more than double any other state); composite 20.3 already at Critical/Developing boundary — additional negative evidence supports moderate downgrade.
methodology_notes: First-ever evidence-based baseline. Live published composite 20.3 (rank 18 US States) sits at the Developing-Critical boundary; the in-window cluster of three negative events plus the structural detention scale supports downward correction crossing the 20-point band boundary. Primary anchor: April 24, 2026 — 5th Circuit vacated the preliminary injunction blocking SB4, ruling plaintiffs lacked standing. SB4 lets state police arrest people suspected of illegal border crossing and lets state magistrate judges order removal to Mexico in lieu of prosecution — a state-level parallel deportation system without federal oversight. This is a structural EQU/BND event: the law authorizes racially-targeted enforcement and summary deportation. EQU drops to 6.3, ACC drops to 6.3, INT drops to 6.3 — all crossing into Critical-band territory consistent with the structural scope of the change. Secondary anchor: April 22, 2026 — Houston City Council voted 13-4 to gut the April 8 sanctuary ordinance after Governor Abbott froze $110M+ in public safety grants. The amended ordinance struck the language declaring administrative warrants 'are not reviewed by a neutral magistrate or judge and are not probable cause for a criminal arrest.' This is an ACC dimension event at the state level (the state coerced a major city into reversing local human rights protections via fiscal threat). Tertiary anchor: Texas holds 18,734 ICE detainees as of February 7, 2026 — more than double any other state (Louisiana 8,244, Georgia 4,227). The state hosts the largest ICE detention center in the nation (Ero El Paso Camp East Montana, 2,505/day average FY2026), and ICE detention deaths are at record pace with one Texas facility 'bearing the brunt' (NPR April 3). BND drops -6.2 reflecting structural facilitation of large-scale detention at Critical scale. Confidence high — three independent in-window structural events plus quantitative detention scale evidence all consistent. Band change Developing → Critical is calibrated (e.g., Florida live composite 18.4 sits in Critical band with comparable but less severe profile; Texas's combination of (a) state-level deportation law, (b) state coercion of cities, and (c) largest detention scale in nation justifies sub-15 composite). Note: positive structural anchors (large healthcare sector, energy industry employment, public university system, port commerce) are insufficient to offset civil-rights regression at this scale; they support the AWR/ACT/EMP/SYS modest 18.8 floor (vs absolute Critical floor of ~5).

evidence.claim: April 24, 2026: 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals vacated the preliminary injunction blocking Texas SB4, ruling plaintiffs lacked standing. SB4 lets state police arrest people suspected of illegal border crossing and lets magistrate judges order removal to Mexico in lieu of prosecution — state-level parallel deportation system. Texas Tribune confirms decision and quotes order: 'We vacate the preliminary injunction to the contrary.' Las Americas, American Gateways, and city of El Paso were challenging plaintiffs.

April 22, 2026: Houston City Council voted 13-4 to amend (gut) sanctuary ordinance after Governor Abbott office threatened to withdraw $110M in public safety grants on April 13. Mayor Whitmire — who originally voted for the April 8 ordinance — pushed for special meeting to repeal. Revised ordinance struck definition declaring civil immigration warrants 'are not reviewed by a neutral magistrate or judge and are not probable cause for a criminal arrest.' Travis Fife (Texas Civil Rights Project) confirmed the revisions 'gut the ordinance.'

Texas held 18,734 ICE detainees as of February 7, 2026 — more than double any other state (Louisiana 8,244, Georgia 4,227). The state hosts the nation's largest ICE detention center (Ero El Paso Camp East Montana) averaging 2,505 detainees/day in FY 2026. NPR reports ICE detention deaths at record pace with one Texas facility 'bearing the brunt.'
NPR April 3, 2026: ICE detention deaths on record pace, one Texas facility bears the brunt — quantitative outcome harm tracked at federal-watchdog level.

Texas AG Ken Paxton filed lawsuit against Houston Mayor and City Council over the April 8 sanctuary ordinance — state-level legal apparatus deployed against municipal human rights protections, prior to the April 22 capitulation.

Houston Public Media confirms Houston Police Department revisions allow detention of individuals on ICE administrative warrants alone (no judicial warrant required) — in conjunction with Palantir-generated immigration warrants documented in The Intercept April 24, this completes the cascade from federal surveillance infrastructure to state law to local enforcement.

key_evidence: April 24 5th Circuit ruling vacates SB4 injunction — state-level parallel deportation system activated, no federal oversight
April 22 Houston ordinance gutted (13-4 vote) under Abbott $110M+ funding coercion — state fiscal coercion overrides local human rights protection
Texas holds 18,734 ICE detainees (Feb 7, 2026) — more than double any other state; largest ICE detention center in nation in El Paso averaging 2,505/day
ICE detention deaths at record pace, one Texas facility 'bearing the brunt' (NPR April 3)
Texas AG Paxton sued Houston mayor and council over original ordinance — state legal apparatus deployed against municipal protections
Live composite 20.3 sits at Developing-Critical boundary; cluster of in-window structural events justifies crossing into Critical band
data_integrity_flag: Scanner queue and rotation-state.json reflected Texas at composite 15.3 rank 48. LIVE site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json shows Texas at composite 20.3 rank 18 (Developing band). Per assessor instruction, live composites are deterministic. Recommend rotation-state reconciliation pass.
